@indexing.status.ariaLabel.description: Accessibility label for the small hourglass icon in the top-right corner that shows while Cmdr is indexing a drive. Read aloud by screen readers; the live progress is in its tooltip.

indexing.drive.heading: {name}

@indexing.drive.heading.description: Heading naming which drive a block in the indexing tooltip is about, always shown above that drive''s status so the user can tell which drive is indexing (e.g. 'Macintosh HD', 'Backups', a phone''s name). {name} is the drive''s display name; this is just the name on its own line, styled as a heading.

placeholders.name: the drive''s display name (e.g. Macintosh HD, Backups)

indexing.scan.label: Scanning your drive...

@indexing.scan.label.description: Generic scanning fallback shown on the drive''s badge for ANY scan (first or repeat) before live progress activity arrives. The trailing '...' signals ongoing work; keep it.

@indexing.scan.counters.description: Standalone running tally detail line shown under the scan heading, e.g. '12,345 entries, 678 dirs'. No leading space (it''s its own line, not joined to the heading); 'dirs' is short for directories/folders (keep it terse, this is a compact status line).

placeholders.entriesText: number of files and folders scanned so far, already formatted (e.g. 12,345)
placeholders.dirsText: number of folders found so far, already formatted (e.g. 678)

@indexing.scan.countersElapsed.description: Detail line under the heading during a drive''s FIRST scan, joining the running entry/dir tally with how long the scan has been running, e.g. '12,345 entries, 678 dirs · 2:34'. There''s no trustworthy percent on a first scan, so this count + elapsed clock is the honest progress signal instead of a bar. The middle dot (·) is a separator; keep it. {counters} and {elapsed} are already formatted (don''t translate inside them); place the separator idiomatically for your language.

placeholders.counters: the already-formatted entry/dir tally (e.g. '12,345 entries, 678 dirs')
placeholders.elapsed: how long the scan has been running, as a clock (minutes:seconds, e.g. 2:34)

@indexing.scan.etaRough.description: Wraps the estimated time remaining during a rough first scan to flag it as approximate, e.g. 'roughly 2m left'. {eta} is an already-formatted time-left phrase; don't translate inside it.

placeholders.eta: the formatted time-remaining phrase, e.g. '2m left'

@indexing.run.firstScan.description: Run-kind header at the top of the drive-indexing checklist when Cmdr scans this drive for the very first time (no earlier index exists). Tells the user this is the big one-time pass, not a routine update. Short noun phrase, sentence case, no trailing period.

@indexing.run.rescan.description: Run-kind header at the top of the drive-indexing checklist when Cmdr re-walks the whole drive even though an index already exists (a stale index, a change-log gap, and similar). Distinguishes this full pass from the quick update. Short noun phrase, sentence case, no trailing period.

@indexing.run.update.description: Run-kind header at the top of the drive-indexing checklist when Cmdr only replays recorded file changes instead of re-walking the drive (the light alternative to a full scan). Reassures the user this is the fast path. Short noun phrase, sentence case, no trailing period.

@indexing.step.findFiles.description: Checklist step label in the drive-indexing tooltip for the scan that walks the drive and finds every file and folder. The user sees it tick from waiting, to in progress (a spinner, with a live count under it), to done (a check). Short imperative label; sentence case; no trailing period or ellipsis (the spinner signals ongoing work, not the text).

@indexing.step.saveFileList.description: Checklist step label in the drive-indexing tooltip for writing the scanned file list into Cmdr''s index. Shown only for a local drive (network drives save as they scan, so this step doesn''t appear). Avoid the word ''entries'' (a database term) and ''build the index'' (the whole operation is indexing). Short, sentence case, no trailing period.

@indexing.step.computeFolderSizes.description: Checklist step label in the drive-indexing tooltip for working out the total size of every folder from the index. Use ''folders'' (not ''directories'') to match the sub-phase lines under it. Short, sentence case, no trailing period.

@indexing.step.catchUp.description: Checklist step label in the drive-indexing tooltip for the final pass that applies file changes that happened while the scan was running. Shown only for a local drive. Avoid the jargon ''reconcile''. Short, friendly, sentence case, no trailing period.

@indexing.step.updateIndex.description: Checklist step label in the drive-indexing tooltip when Cmdr is replaying recorded file changes to bring its index up to date (the lighter alternative to a full scan). This is the only step shown for that quicker update. ''index'' is established Cmdr vocabulary here. Short, sentence case, no trailing period.

@indexing.summary.found.description: Compact progress metric for a SECONDARY drive in the indexing tooltip when several drives index at once (the primary drive shows the full checklist; the others collapse to one line: drive name + current step + this metric). Shown during a first scan that has no trustworthy percent yet, so it leads with the honest running count of files and folders found, e.g. ''171,607 found''. {countText} is already formatted; keep it terse.

placeholders.countText: number of files and folders found so far, already formatted (e.g. 171,607)

indexing.step.findFilesFirstScan: First scan, so this can take a while

@indexing.step.findFilesFirstScan.description: Reassuring sub-line under the active ''Find files'' checklist step the very FIRST time Cmdr scans a drive (no prior scan, so there''s no trustworthy time estimate). Sets the expectation that a first scan is slow, so the climbing count and clock don''t read as stuck. Calm and friendly; sentence case; no trailing period.

@indexing.step.statusDone.description: Visually-hidden status word a screen reader announces after a finished checklist step (e.g. ''Find files. Done.''). It conveys the checkmark to non-sighted users. Very short; sentence case; no period.

@indexing.step.statusActive.description: Visually-hidden status phrase a screen reader announces after the checklist step that''s currently running (e.g. ''Compute folder sizes. In progress.''). It conveys the spinner to non-sighted users. Very short; sentence case; no period.

@indexing.step.statusPending.description: Visually-hidden status phrase a screen reader announces after a checklist step that hasn''t started yet (e.g. ''Catch up on recent changes. Not started.''). It conveys the empty marker to non-sighted users. Very short; sentence case; no period.

@indexing.aggregation.loading.description: Sub-line under the active ''Compute folder sizes'' checklist step while Cmdr loads folders from the index before sizing them. Use ''folders'' (not ''directories''). Trailing '...' signals ongoing work; keep it.

@indexing.aggregation.sorting.description: Sub-line under the active ''Compute folder sizes'' checklist step while Cmdr sorts folders before sizing them. Use ''folders'' (not ''directories''). Trailing '...' signals ongoing work; keep it.

@indexing.aggregation.computing.description: Sub-line under the active ''Compute folder sizes'' checklist step while Cmdr adds up each folder''s total size. Also the fallback for any unrecognized compute sub-phase. Use ''folders'' (not ''directories''). Trailing '...' signals ongoing work; keep it.

@indexing.aggregation.writing.description: Sub-line under the active ''Compute folder sizes'' checklist step while Cmdr writes the computed folder sizes back into its index. Use ''folders'' (not ''directories''). Trailing '...' signals ongoing work; keep it.

@indexing.replay.detail.description: Tooltip detail line under the 'Updating index' heading, e.g. '1,234 events processed'. 'events' means recorded filesystem changes.

placeholders.eventsText: number of change events processed so far, already formatted (e.g. 1,234)

@indexing.progress.percentEta.description: Compact progress line in the indexing tooltip joining the percent complete with the time remaining, e.g. '95%, roughly 8s left'. {eta} is an already-formatted time-left phrase (don't translate inside it); place the comma idiomatically for your language.

placeholders.eta: the formatted time-remaining phrase, e.g. 'roughly 8s left'

@indexing.eta.almostDone.description: Time-remaining phrase shown when an indexing operation has under two seconds left (a precise countdown there would be noise). Reassuring and terse.

@indexing.eta.secondsLeft.description: Estimated time remaining under a minute, e.g. '45s left'. The 's' is the abbreviation for seconds; keep this very short (it sits in a compact tooltip).

@indexing.eta.minutesLeft.description: Estimated time remaining between a minute and an hour, e.g. '3m left'. The 'm' is the abbreviation for minutes; keep this very short (it sits in a compact tooltip).

indexing.eta.hoursMinutesLeft: {hours, plural, one {{hoursText} hour} other {{hoursText} hours}} {minutes, plural, one {{minutesText} minute} other {{minutesText} minutes}} left

@indexing.eta.hoursMinutesLeft.description: Estimated time remaining between one and ten hours, spelled out so an hour-scale wait is readable at a glance, e.g. '1 hour 24 minutes left' (never '84m left'). Shown only when the minutes part is non-zero; otherwise the hours-only message is used.

placeholders.hours: raw whole hours remaining, drives the hour plural
placeholders.minutes: raw whole minutes past the hour, drives the minute plural
placeholders.minutesText: whole minutes past the hour, already formatted (e.g. 24)

indexing.eta.hoursLeft: {hours, plural, one {{hoursText} hour} other {{hoursText} hours}} left

@indexing.eta.hoursLeft.description: Estimated time remaining in whole hours: an exact hour count under ten hours ('2 hours left'), or any wait of ten hours and up rounded to the nearest hour ('20 hours left') because minute precision is noise at that scale.

placeholders.hours: raw whole hours remaining, drives the plural

indexing.rescan.staleIndex: Your drive index is outdated. It looks like the app hasn''t run for a while. Running a fresh scan to catch up.

@indexing.rescan.staleIndex.description: Info toast explaining why Cmdr is rescanning the drive: the index went stale because the app wasn't open for a while. Calm and explanatory.

indexing.rescan.journalGap: The system''s file change log doesn''t go back far enough. Running a fresh scan to rebuild the index.

@indexing.rescan.journalGap.description: Info toast explaining why Cmdr is rescanning: the macOS file-change journal no longer covers the gap since last run. Calm and explanatory.

indexing.rescan.replayOverflow: A lot of file changes happened since last run. Running a fresh scan instead of replaying them one by one.

@indexing.rescan.replayOverflow.description: Info toast explaining why Cmdr is doing a full rescan rather than replaying changes: too many changes accumulated since last run. Calm and explanatory.

indexing.rescan.watcherStartFailed: Couldn''t start the file change watcher. Running a fresh scan to get the index up to date.

@indexing.rescan.watcherStartFailed.description: Info toast explaining why Cmdr is rescanning: the live file-change watcher couldn't start, so it falls back to a full scan. Calm and explanatory; avoid the words 'error' and 'failed'.

indexing.rescan.reconcilerBufferOverflow: Heavy filesystem activity overwhelmed the event buffer. Running a fresh scan to stay accurate.

@indexing.rescan.reconcilerBufferOverflo.description: Info toast explaining why Cmdr is rescanning: a burst of filesystem activity overflowed an internal buffer. Calm and explanatory.

indexing.rescan.incompletePreviousScan: The previous scan didn''t finish (the app may have been closed). Restarting the scan from scratch.

@indexing.rescan.incompletePreviousScan.description: Info toast explaining why Cmdr is rescanning: the last scan didn't complete (the app likely closed mid-scan). Calm and explanatory.

indexing.rescan.watcherChannelOverflow: A burst of filesystem activity overflowed the watcher channel. Running a fresh scan to stay accurate.

@indexing.rescan.watcherChannelOverflow.description: Info toast explaining why Cmdr is rescanning: a burst of filesystem activity overflowed the watcher's internal channel. Calm and explanatory.

indexing.rescan.fallback: Running a fresh drive scan to keep the index accurate.

@indexing.rescan.fallback.description: Generic fallback info toast shown when Cmdr rescans the drive for a reason not covered by the specific messages. Calm and explanatory.

indexing.firstConnect.title: Index {name}?

@indexing.firstConnect.title.description: Heading of the notification shown the first time the user opens a new external drive (a network share or device). Asks whether to index it for folder sizes and search.

indexing.firstConnect.body: Cmdr can index this drive in the background so folder sizes and search work here. It only runs while the drive is connected.

@indexing.firstConnect.body.description: Body of the first-connect indexing notification, explaining what indexing the drive gives you and that it only runs while connected.

@indexing.firstConnect.enable.description: Primary button in the first-connect notification. Turns on indexing for this drive and starts the scan.

indexing.firstConnect.silenceDrive: Don''t ask again for this drive

@indexing.firstConnect.silenceDrive.description: Button in the first-connect notification that stops Cmdr asking about THIS drive again (but keeps asking about others).

indexing.firstConnect.silenceAll: Don''t ask again for any drives

@indexing.firstConnect.silenceAll.description: Button in the first-connect notification that stops Cmdr asking about indexing for ANY drive (turns the per-drive prompt off in Settings).

indexing.staleDialog.title: This drive''s index may be out of date

@indexing.staleDialog.title.description: Title of the one-time dialog shown the first time any external drive''s index goes stale (the drive was disconnected, so changes may have been missed). Never use the words ''error'' or ''failed''.

indexing.staleDialog.body: While {name} was disconnected, files on it may have changed. Folder sizes and search results might be a bit off until you rescan. The yellow status next to the drive always shows when this happens, and a rescan refreshes it.

@indexing.staleDialog.body.description: Body of the one-time stale-drive dialog, explaining that the drive may have changed while disconnected, that the yellow status flags it, and that a rescan refreshes it. Calm and reassuring; active voice.

@indexing.staleDialog.neverShowAgain.description: Button in the one-time stale-drive dialog that turns off this notification for good (the yellow status still shows). Sentence case.

@indexing.staleDialog.close.description: Button in the one-time stale-drive dialog that dismisses it without changing the notification setting.

indexing.enrich.label: Image indexing

@indexing.enrich.label.description: Row title in the top-right indexing tooltip for image content-indexing (Cmdr reading images so they can be searched by what's in them), shown above the progress bar. Sits alongside the drive-indexing rows. Sentence case, terse.

indexing.enrich.progress: {done, plural, one {{doneText} of {totalText} image} other {{doneText} of {totalText} images}}

@indexing.enrich.progress.description: Running count under the Image-indexing row, e.g. '1,200 of 5,000 images'. {doneText}/{totalText} are already-formatted numbers; {done} drives the plural. No trailing words (a separate rate/ETA line follows).

@indexing.enrich.rate.description: Processing speed shown beside the Image-indexing progress, e.g. '320/min' meaning 320 images per minute. Keep it compact; '/min' is per minute.

@indexing.enrich.rateEta.description: Joins the speed and the time-left into one detail line, e.g. '320/min · 2m left'. The middle dot is a separator; keep spaces around it.

@indexing.enrich.pausedIdle.description: Status shown for a network (NAS) drive whose image indexing paused because you're actively using the app; it resumes when you stop. Reassuring, not an error.

@indexing.enrich.pausedDisconnected.description: Status shown for a network drive whose image indexing paused because the drive disconnected; it resumes automatically when the drive reconnects. Reassuring, not an error. No em-dash.

indexing.enrich.queued: Image indexing starts after the drive scan

@indexing.enrich.queued.description: Quiet one-liner at the bottom of the drive-indexing tooltip when image indexing is turned on but its pass is waiting for the drive scan above it to finish (it starts automatically right after). Gives the ''Index image contents'' toggle immediate visible feedback. Calm, sentence case, no trailing period.

@indexing.enrich.imagesBarLabel.description: Accessible label (screen-reader only) for the images progress bar in the Image-indexing row. Not shown visually.

@indexing.enrich.bytesBarLabel.description: Accessible label (screen-reader only) for the second progress bar, which tracks how much image DATA (bytes) has been processed. Not shown visually.
